tasks.title: Create Configuration File Structure and Loading Utilities
tasks.description: Set up config directory with fastagent.config.yaml, fastagent.secrets.yaml (gitignored), and approval_rules.yaml templates with environment variable substitution support.
tasks.details:
Create config/ directory. Use PyYAML 6.0.2 for parsing. Implement config loader with python-dotenv 1.0.1 for env var substitution (${VAR_NAME}). Add validation with pydantic 2.9.2. Template fastagent.config.yaml with MCP servers list, model='claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022', max_tokens=4096. fastagent.secrets.yaml with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN, DISCORD_TOKEN placeholders. approval_rules.yaml with tool_name: true/false mappings. Add .gitignore for secrets. Pseudo-code: ```python
def load_config(path): with open(path) as f: data = yaml.safe_load(f) return substitute_env_vars(data)```
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: verify YAML parsing, env substitution (dotenv), validation passes/fails, defaults applied. Mock env vars, assert loaded dict structure matches schema. Coverage: 90%+.

tasks.title: Implement PlatformInterface Abstract Base Class
tasks.description: Create connectors/interface.py with PlatformInterface ABC and PlatformMessage dataclass for standardized message handling.
tasks.details:
Use abc module and dataclasses. Define PlatformMessage with thread_id:str, user_id:str, content:str, platform:str, channel_id:str, is_mention:bool. Abstract methods: async receive_message(event), async send_message(thread_id, content, msg_type), async request_approval(thread_id, tool_name, params, timeout), async handle_button_click(payload). Use typing annotations. Pseudo-code: ```python
class PlatformInterface(ABC): @abstractmethod async def send_message(self, ...): pass``` Export PlatformMessage, PlatformInterface.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: verify ABC cannot instantiate, dataclass serialization/deserialization, type checking. Mock subclass implementation tests.

tasks.title: Install and Verify fast-agent-mcp Framework Integration
tasks.description: Install fast-agent-mcp and create minimal POC agent with MCP server connection and basic tool execution.

Create test_agent.py with @fast.agent(model='claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022', mcp_servers=[{'url': 'ws://mcp-server1'}]). Test agent.send('test message'). Verify tool discovery from 5-6 MCP servers. POC: minimal ToolRunnerHook that logs tool calls. Confirm async hook support and exception blocking.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: agent instantiation, MCP connection (mock servers), tool discovery (assert 90+ tools), basic message processing, hook interception. Verify no tool calls without approval simulation.

tasks.title: Implement ApprovalRulesManager
tasks.description: Create approval/rules_manager.py to load and query tool approval rules from approval_rules.yaml.
tasks.details:
Use PyYAML 6.0.2, pydantic for validation. Class ApprovalRulesManager with load_rules(path), requires_approval(tool_name) -> bool (default False). Support tool categories: {'filesystem/*': true}. Cache loaded rules. Pseudo-code: ```python
class ApprovalRulesManager: def __init__(self, config_path): self.rules = self._load_yaml(config_path) def requires_approval(self, tool): return self.rules.get(tool, False)```
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests: rule loading, exact match, category match (glob 'filesystem/read'), missing tool default False, invalid YAML graceful fallback, cache hit/miss.

tasks.title: Implement ApprovalTracker System

Methods: create_approval(thread_id, tool_name, params, timeout=300) -> approval_id, resolve_approval(approval_id, approved:bool), get_future(approval_id) -> Future[bool]. Timeout via asyncio.wait_for. Cleanup expired. Dataclass ToolApprovalRequest(approval_id, thread_id, tool_name, tool_input:dict, tool_use_id:str, created_at:datetime, future:Future).

tasks.title: Create agent/agent_config.py and ChatPlatformToolAgent
tasks.description: Define fast-agent with MCP config and custom ChatPlatformToolAgent using ToolRunnerHooks for approval integration.
tasks.details: agent/agent_config.py: def create_agent() -> ToolAgent. Use @fast.agent from config. agent/tool_agent.py: class ChatPlatformToolAgent(ToolAgent, ToolRunnerHookCapable): Implement ToolRunnerHooks interface methods - specifically async def before_tool_call(self, tool_use_id, tool_name, tool_input) and async def after_tool_call(self, tool_use_id, tool_name, tool_input, result). In before_tool_call: if rules.requires(tool_name): approval_id = tracker.create(...); approved = await tracker.get_future(approval_id); if not approved: raise ToolApprovalDenied(). Integrate platform_interface.send_status() for status updates.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests: agent creation, MCP tool discovery, ToolRunnerHooks interception (mock platform/rules/tracker), approval block via exception, result reporting. Verify both before_tool_call and after_tool_call hooks are properly invoked in the fast-agent tool execution flow.

subtasks.title: Define ChatPlatformToolAgent base class
subtasks.description: Create agent/tool_agent.py with class ChatPlatformToolAgent(ToolAgent, ToolRunnerHookCapable) implementing ToolRunnerHooks interface for tool execution interception.
subtasks.details:
Inherit from ToolAgent and ToolRunnerHookCapable. Initialize platform_interface in __init__. Implement the ToolRunnerHooks interface methods (before_tool_call and after_tool_call) from fast-agent framework's hook system. Add async methods for status messaging via platform_interface.send_status().
<info added on 2026-01-05T12:13:14.423Z>
ToolRunnerHookCapable is a specific interface from the fast-agent framework that enables tool execution interception. It requires implementing the ToolRunnerHooks interface methods (before_tool_call and after_tool_call), which are called during the framework's tool execution flow. These hooks are distinct from other hook patterns like PreToolUse/PostToolUse and are specifically designed to integrate with fast-agent's tool execution pipeline. When implementing ChatPlatformToolAgent, these hooks provide the critical interception points needed for approval flows and status messaging to be properly integrated with the fast-agent framework's execution model.
</info added on 2026-01-05T12:13:14.423Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test: instantiation, inheritance verification, ToolRunnerHookCapable interface implementation, platform_interface integration mock.

subtasks.title: Implement before_tool_call hook method
subtasks.description: Add async def before_tool_call(self, tool_use_id, tool_name, tool_input) with rules check, tracker approval, and exception on denial as part of ToolRunnerHooks interface.
subtasks.details:
Implement the before_tool_call method from ToolRunnerHookCapable interface. Check if rules.requires(tool_name), create approval_id = tracker.create(...), await approved = tracker.get_future(approval_id), raise ToolApprovalDenied() if not approved. Call platform_interface.send_status() for updates.
<info added on 2026-01-05T12:13:21.186Z>
Implement the before_tool_call method as part of the fast-agent ToolRunnerHooks interface. This method is called by the fast-agent framework before a tool is executed, not to be confused with any PreToolUse hooks mentioned elsewhere. The method signature follows the ToolRunnerHookCapable interface specification:

```python
async def before_tool_call(self, tool_use_id: str, tool_name: str, tool_input: dict) -> None:
    """Hook called by fast-agent framework before tool execution"""
    if self.rules.requires_approval(tool_name):
        approval_id = self.tracker.create(
            thread_id=self.thread_id,
            tool_name=tool_name,
            tool_input=tool_input,
            tool_use_id=tool_use_id
        )
        
        # Send status update that approval is pending
        await self.platform_interface.send_status(
            thread_id=self.thread_id,
            message=f"Waiting for approval to use tool: {tool_name}"
        )
        
        # Wait for approval decision
        try:
            approved = await self.tracker.get_future(approval_id)
            if not approved:
                raise ToolApprovalDenied(f"Use of tool '{tool_name}' was denied")
        except asyncio.TimeoutError:
            raise ToolApprovalDenied(f"Approval for tool '{tool_name}' timed out")
```
</info added on 2026-01-05T12:13:21.186Z>
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test: mock rules/tracker/platform, verify ToolRunnerHooks hook interception, approval flow (approve/deny), exception raised on denial.

tasks.title: Implement SlackConnector
tasks.description: Create connectors/slack_connector.py implementing PlatformInterface for Slack events, messages, approvals, buttons.
tasks.details:
slack-bolt 1.19.2. Handle app_mention, message, block_actions. Normalize to PlatformMessage (event['ts'] as thread_id). send_message: say() with blocks/embeds. request_approval: post ephemeral with buttons (approve/deny actions). handle_button_click: ack(), update tracker. Integrate factoids.check_message(). Pseudo-code: ```python
class SlackConnector(PlatformInterface): async def handle_event(self, body, say): msg = PlatformMessage(...) if factoids.match(msg): await say(factoids.response); return await self.agent.process(msg)```

tasks.title: Implement Core MessageHandler Class for Centralized Agent Communication
tasks.description: Create a core MessageHandler class that centralizes all agent.send() calls, receives normalized PlatformMessages from connectors, and routes responses back through the appropriate connector.
tasks.details:
1. Create a new file `core/message_handler.py` that will serve as the central point for all agent communication.

2. Implement the `MessageHandler` class with the following structure:
```python
class MessageHandler:
    def __init__(self, agent):
        self.agent = agent
        self.connectors = {}  # Map of platform name to connector instance
    
    def register_connector(self, platform_name, connector):
        """Register a connector for a specific platform"""
        self.connectors[platform_name] = connector
        
    async def handle_message(self, message: PlatformMessage):
        """
        Central method for processing all incoming messages:
        1. Set thread context on agent
        2. Call agent.send() with message content
        3. Route response back through appropriate connector
        """
        # Set thread context (for maintaining conversation state)
        self.agent.set_thread(message.thread_id)
        
        # Process message with agent
        response = await self.agent.send(message.content)
        
        # Route response back through the originating connector
        if message.platform in self.connectors:
            await self.connectors[message.platform].send_message(
                thread_id=message.thread_id,
                content=response,
                msg_type="response"
            )
        else:
            # Log error if connector not found
            logging.error(f"No connector registered for platform: {message.platform}")
            
        return response
```

3. Update `app.py` to initialize and use the MessageHandler:
```python
async def main():
    config = load_config()
    
    # Initialize agent
    agent = create_agent()
    
    # Initialize message handler with agent
    message_handler = MessageHandler(agent)
    
    # Initialize connectors
    slack = SlackConnector(config)
    discord = DiscordConnector(config)
    
    # Register connectors with message handler
    message_handler.register_connector("slack", slack)
    message_handler.register_connector("discord", discord)
    
    # Set message handler on connectors
    slack.message_handler = message_handler
    discord.message_handler = message_handler
    
    # Start application
    app.start()
```

4. Update connector classes to use the MessageHandler instead of directly calling agent.send():
```python
# In SlackConnector
async def handle_event(self, body, say):
    msg = PlatformMessage(
        thread_id=body['event']['ts'],
        user_id=body['event']['user'],
        content=body['event']['text'],
        platform="slack",
        channel_id=body['event']['channel'],
        is_mention=True if 'app_mention' in body['event_type'] else False
    )
    
    # Check for factoids first
    if factoid_response := factoids.check(msg):
        await say(factoid_response)
        return
        
    # Forward to message handler
    await self.message_handler.handle_message(msg)
```

5. Add thread context management to the agent class if not already present:
```python
# In ChatPlatformToolAgent
def set_thread(self, thread_id):
    """Set the current thread context for conversation state"""
    self.current_thread = thread_id
    # Any additional thread context setup
```

6. Ensure proper error handling and logging throughout the MessageHandler implementation.

7. Consider adding support for message metadata and special message types (e.g., ephemeral messages, direct messages) in the handler.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit tests:
   - Test MessageHandler initialization and connector registration
   - Test handle_message with mocked agent and connectors
   - Verify thread context is properly set on the agent
   - Test error handling when platform is not registered
   - Test message routing to the correct connector

2. Integration tests:
   - Test end-to-end flow from connector → MessageHandler → agent → connector
   - Verify that responses are correctly routed back through the originating connector
   - Test with multiple concurrent messages from different platforms
   - Test with different message types (regular messages, mentions, DMs)

3. Mock tests:
   - Create mock PlatformMessage objects for different scenarios
   - Mock agent.send() to return predictable responses
   - Verify connector.send_message() is called with correct parameters

4. Regression tests:
   - Ensure existing functionality (factoids, approvals) still works with the new architecture
   - Verify that thread context is maintained across multiple messages in the same thread

5. Performance tests:
   - Measure latency impact of the additional abstraction layer
   - Test with concurrent messages to ensure no bottlenecks

6. Manual testing:
   - Test with actual Slack and Discord connections
   - Verify responses are correctly formatted and delivered to the right platform and thread

subtasks.title: Create MessageHandler class file and basic structure
subtasks.description: Create the new file core/message_handler.py and implement the basic MessageHandler class with __init__ and register_connector methods as specified.
subtasks.details: Implement class MessageHandler with self.agent and self.connectors dict. Add register_connector method to map platform_name to connector instance. Include proper docstrings and type hints.
subtasks.testStrategy: Unit test initialization with agent mock and connector registration for multiple platforms.

subtasks.title: Implement core handle_message method
subtasks.description: Add the async handle_message method that processes PlatformMessage: sets thread context, calls agent.send(), and routes response back via connector.
subtasks.details: Implement logic to set agent thread context, await agent.send(message.content), then send response through connectors[message.platform].send_message with thread_id, content, msg_type='response'.
subtasks.testStrategy: Mock PlatformMessage, agent.send(), connector.send_message(). Verify thread set, response routing, and return value.

subtasks.title: Update app.py for MessageHandler initialization and connector registration
subtasks.description: Modify app.py main() function to create MessageHandler with agent, initialize/register SlackConnector and DiscordConnector, and set message_handler on connectors.
subtasks.details: Add MessageHandler(agent) instantiation, register_connector('slack', slack), register_connector('discord', discord), and set slack.message_handler/discord.message_handler.
subtasks.testStrategy: Integration test verifying connectors properly registered and message_handler references set correctly.

subtasks.title: Refactor connectors to use MessageHandler instead of direct agent calls
subtasks.description: Update SlackConnector and DiscordConnector handle_event methods to create PlatformMessage and call self.message_handler.handle_message(msg) instead of direct agent.send().
subtasks.details: Preserve existing factoid checks before forwarding to handler. Ensure PlatformMessage properly normalized with all required fields (thread_id, content, platform, etc.).
subtasks.testStrategy: Test connector event handling end-to-end: event→PlatformMessage→MessageHandler→agent→response routing.

subtasks.title: Add agent thread context management, error handling, and logging
subtasks.description: Implement agent.set_thread() method if missing, add comprehensive error handling and logging in MessageHandler, including connector-not-found cases and exceptions.
subtasks.details: Add try/except blocks around agent.send() and connector.send_message(). Use logging.error for missing connectors. Ensure agent has set_thread(current_thread=thread_id) method.
subtasks.testStrategy: Test error scenarios: unregistered platform, agent.send() failures, connector.send_message() exceptions. Verify logs and graceful degradation.
